title: Some customer phone numbers are wrong in TaskFlow
body: We noticed that some of our customer contact records have incorrect phone numbers. The numbers look truncated or mangled. Our support team caught it because they tried calling a few customers and got wrong numbers. We have about 2,000 customer records and I'd estimate maybe 50-100 have wrong numbers. This is a serious data integrity issue for us.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for flagging this — data integrity issues like this are definitely worth investigating carefully.

To help me understand the full picture: can you walk me through how these customer phone numbers typically get into TaskFlow? Are they entered manually by your team, imported from another system, or synced from somewhere? And do you have a sense of whether these numbers were *ever* correct in TaskFlow, or whether they may have come in wrong from the start?

If you happen to have an example or two of what a mangled number looks like compared to what it should be, that would also be really helpful.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
The numbers come from our Salesforce CRM — we have a nightly batch import that syncs customer data into TaskFlow. The numbers were definitely correct before, our support team has been using them for months without issues. I think the problem started maybe 2 weeks ago? Not 100% sure on the exact date but it was around then.

As for examples — I noticed that some of our international customers' numbers look wrong. Like I think one customer in Ireland had a number starting with +353 but now it shows +35 and then the rest of the number. A US customer with +1 looked fine though. I haven't done a full audit but it seems like it's the international numbers that are messed up, at least the ones with longer country codes.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's a really helpful observation about the international numbers — the pattern of +353 becoming +35 is a strong clue. It sounds like something might be cutting off the numbers at a certain length or mishandling the country code parsing.

Can you tell me what changed around two weeks ago? For example, was there an update to TaskFlow, a change to the batch import configuration, or any modifications on the Salesforce side (new fields, changed formats, a Salesforce update)? Even something that seemed unrelated at the time could be the trigger.

Also, I'm curious about the truncation itself — do the affected numbers all end up being roughly the same total length, or does it vary? For instance, is it just the country code portion that gets clipped, or does the rest of the number get shortened too?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
I'm not sure exactly what changed — I think there was some kind of update to the import process? One of our developers mentioned something about updating how phone numbers are handled, maybe to better support international formats or something like that. I don't know the specifics though, I'd have to check with the team. Nothing changed on the Salesforce side as far as I know.

As for the truncation, it seems like it's just the country code part that gets cut off. The rest of the number after the country code looks normal. So like the Ireland example, +353 became +35 but the local number part after it seemed to still be there. I haven't checked a ton of records though so I could be wrong about that.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"International phone numbers truncated during Salesforce batch import — country codes clipped to 2 characters","problem":"Approximately 50-100 of ~2,000 customer phone numbers imported from Salesforce via nightly batch sync have truncated country codes. International numbers with country codes longer than 2 digits are affected (e.g., Ireland +353 becomes +35), while shorter codes like US +1 appear unaffected. The local number portion after the country code remains intact.","root_cause_hypothesis":"A recent change (~2 weeks ago) to the phone number import/parsing logic is likely truncating the country code portion of international phone numbers to a fixed length of 2 characters. This could be a hard-coded field width, an incorrect substring/slice operation on the country code, or a regex that captures only the first 2 digits after the '+' sign.","reproduction_steps":["Identify the Salesforce batch import job and its phone number parsing logic","Find the code change from approximately 2 weeks ago that modified phone number handling","Run the import against a test record with a 3-digit country code (e.g., +353 for Ireland)","Compare the stored value in TaskFlow against the source value in Salesforce","Verify that 1-digit (+1) and 2-digit (+44) country codes are unaffected or affected"],"environment":"Salesforce CRM → nightly batch import → TaskFlow. Issue is in the import/parsing layer, not in Salesforce data or TaskFlow storage/display.","severity":"high","impact":"Support team cannot reach international customers by phone. ~50-100 records currently corrupted. Data corruption is ongoing with each nightly import, so the affected count may grow. Existing correct records may be overwritten with truncated values on re-import.","recommended_fix":"1. Identify and revert or fix the recent change to phone number parsing in the batch import code. 2. Ensure the country code field (or parsing logic) supports up to 3-digit country codes per the ITU E.164 standard. 3. Write a migration/repair script to re-import affected phone numbers from Salesforce to restore correct values. 4. Consider adding a validation check that flags phone numbers whose country code doesn't match a known valid code.","proposed_test_case":"Unit test the phone number parser with representative international formats: +1 (1-digit), +44 (2-digit), +353 (3-digit), and +1-868 (3-digit Caribbean). Assert that the full country code and local number are preserved. Add an integration test that imports a batch containing mixed country code lengths and verifies no truncation occurs.","information_gaps":["Exact commit or PR that changed the phone number handling logic (~2 weeks ago)","Whether 2-digit country codes (e.g., UK +44) are also affected or only 3-digit codes","Whether previously-correct records are being re-corrupted on each nightly import or only new records are affected"]}
